title: Time Warp Attack
description: Time warp attacks manipulate block timestamps across difficulty periods so miners can suppress or lower future difficulty despite faster block production. The exploit depends on Bitcoin's off-by-one retarget bug plus the consensus limits on miner-controlled time, so it sits at the intersection of retarget math and timestamp rules.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech Topic: Time Warp
resources.notes: Canonical overview of the attack, why it matters, and the current mitigation discussion.

resources.title: BIP 54: Consensus Cleanup
resources.notes: Specification and motivation for fixing the time warp bug as part of the consensus cleanup bundle.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech Newsletter #299
resources.notes: Explains the off-by-one retarget bug and why it matters because it enables the time-warp exploit.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Broad technical reference for the mining, block-header, and consensus mechanics that make the exploit legible.
